items.headline: Inflation Concerns and Geopolitical Tensions Weigh on Markets
items.summary: Global equity markets are experiencing a cautious start to the week, with major U.S. indices showing declines in pre-market and early trading. Intensifying inflation fears, driven by surging oil prices and rising Treasury yields, coupled with ongoing geopolitical uncertainties surrounding the U.S.-Iran conflict, are contributing to a risk-off sentiment. Investors are also keenly anticipating key economic data and major earnings reports later this week.

items.statContext: Down 0.38% from previous session. (Friday's close: 7,408.50, down 1.24%)
Futures were down 0.70% in pre-market. (Friday's close: 26,225.15, down 1.54%)
Futures were down 0.93% in pre-market. (Friday's close: 49,526.17, down 1.07%)

items.headline: Regeneron Pharma Tumbles on Trial Results
items.summary: Regeneron Pharmaceuticals ($REGN) experienced a significant decline in pre-market trading, tumbling 11.92% after its Phase 3 trials for metastatic melanoma did not achieve statistical significance.

items.headline: Energy Sector Shows Resilience Amidst Oil Surge
items.summary: While the broader market experienced a sell-off, the energy sector demonstrated strength, collectively rising 2.3% as crude oil prices surged on ongoing supply concerns and geopolitical tensions.

items.headline: April US Inflation Jumps to 3.8%, Fueling Rate Hike Concerns
items.summary: U.S. inflation surged to 3.8% in April, higher than anticipated, primarily driven by the ongoing U.S.-Iran conflict and its impact on energy prices. This data is leading traders to rule out Federal Reserve rate cuts this year and raises prospects for potential future rate hikes.

items.headline: Fed Minutes and Housing Data Expected This Week
items.summary: The Federal Reserve's FOMC meeting minutes, due on Thursday, will be closely scrutinized for signals regarding future interest rate paths, especially after recent inflation data. Additionally, the U.S. May NAHB Housing Market Index and other housing-related indicators are scheduled for release this week.

items.headline: Nvidia Earnings Eyed Amidst Tech Pullback; Walmart & Home Depot Also Reporting
items.summary: Investors are keenly awaiting Nvidia's ($NVDA) earnings report on Tuesday, a critical event for the AI-driven tech sector, which has seen profit-taking. Major retailers like Walmart ($WMT) on Thursday, and Home Depot ($HD), Lowe's ($LOW), and Target ($TGT) earlier in the week, are also slated to report, offering insights into consumer behavior amidst inflation.

items.headline: Oil Prices Surge to Two-Week Highs on Middle East Tensions
items.summary: Crude oil prices have climbed past $110, reaching their highest levels in two weeks, as prospects for an end to the U.S.-Iran conflict remain uncertain following an attack on a nuclear facility in the UAE and President Trump's warnings to Iran. This surge contributes to broader inflation worries.
